label: About
title: About Paper Tailnet Observatory
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 21 recent saved-signal files and 62 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around Article cover image and 0xSero (@0xSero) on X, plus 7 more. A source image from pbs.twimg.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Paper Tailnet Observatory: A fashion/material macro still, built as a side-lit object slab: one long off-white paper-resin surface runs edge to edge in a wide banner crop. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against descending green circuit path with raised circular nodes, dark recessed red-orange dotted network band, and yellow adhesive tape tabs under cable pressure.

typography.body_family: 'DFE Newsreader', Iowan Old Style, Georgia, serif
typography.rationale: Fraunces gives the title an organic, seed-label quality; Newsreader keeps the explanation calm and readable.
